dict.string:
Hither bootstrap re-applier — defends /etc/synthetic.conf and /etc/auto_master against macOS update reverts.
Purpose: Re-apply the `Hither` entry to /etc/synthetic.conf and the `/Hither/{host} hither_{host} -nosuid` indirect-map entries to /etc/auto_master at every boot, and whenever those files are modified at runtime. This survives the macOS minor-update behavior of resetting /etc/auto_master to its shipping default (observed on macOS 15.7.7 wiping previously-installed indirect-map entries).
Context: Runs as root at boot via RunAtLoad. Also runs whenever /etc/synthetic.conf or /etc/auto_master changes (WatchPaths). Performs ONLY local file edits + `apfs.util -t` + `automount -cv`. ZERO network calls — safe to run before networking (Wi-Fi, DHCP, DNS, any overlay VPN) is up. ZERO mount probes (no stat/ls/cat under /Hither/{host}/) — autofs would attempt a root-context mount with no Keychain access, breaking the share.
Dependencies: requires /usr/local/sbin/hither-write-map to be installed (root-owned, mode 0755) and /usr/local/libexec/hither/{add-synthetic-root,apply-auto-master}.sh (root-owned, mode 0755) — the bootstrap scripts that this daemon executes. Requires /etc/sudoers.d/hither-write-map (granting %admin NOPASSWD on the wrapper, regex-restricted to bare hostnames). EnvironmentVariables.PATH is set explicitly because launchd-spawned processes inherit a minimal PATH (/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin); we extend it for the Hither CLI's own internal calls.
Operation: stdout → /var/log/hither/bootstrap.stdout.log; stderr → /var/log/hither/bootstrap.stderr.log. Use `sudo launchctl print system/com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` to inspect state. Use `sudo launchctl kickstart system/com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` to fire manually. Use `sudo launchctl bootout system/com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` to disable.
Note on the Label: `com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` carries the original author's name as the reverse-DNS prefix. This is historical; the Label is baked into existing installs and is not renamed in the v0.x series to avoid breaking upgrade paths. Operators inspecting `launchctl list` will see the johnrandall prefix; this is expected.
References: github.com/johntrandall/hither, docs/architecture.md, docs/design-decisions.md.

array.string: /bin/bash /usr/local/libexec/hither/add-synthetic-root.sh && for sub in /etc/hither_*; do [ -f "$sub" ] || continue; case "$sub" in *.needs-reload) continue;; esac; host=$(basename "$sub" | sed 's/^hither_//'); /bin/bash /usr/local/libexec/hither/apply-auto-master.sh "$host"; done
